data.title: Trump supports a de minimis tax exemption for Bitcoin payments: White House
data.description:

This is to make it easier for Americans to use Bitcoin and other cryptos on a day-to-day basis. Karoline Leavitt revealed that President Trump has already signaled support for the exemption and […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-supports-a-de-minimis-tax-for-bitcoin/

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Advocates for July Rate Cut
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller has suggested that the Federal Reserve should consider a 25 basis point interest rate cut during its July meeting. This recommendation comes amid ongoing discussions about the economic outlook and monetary policy adjustments.

P. , confirming it would continue selling common stock directly to the public, including forward sales, under what’s classified as an at-the-market […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/sharplink-to-raise-6b-for-ether-buying-spree/

data.title: SharpLink plans to sell another $5 billion in shares to increase its Ethereum holdings
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ: SBET), a Minnesota company, submitted an updated prospectus to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), planning to issue an additional $5 billion in common stock on top of the original $1 billion to further purchase Ethereum (ETH) for the company's treasury. As of July 16, the company has held more than 321,000 ETH (about $1.1 billion), making it the largest corporate holder of Ethereum. The expansion follows a $425 million private placement led by Consensys in June, where Consensys CEO and Ethereum co-founder Joseph Lubin now serves as chairman of the board of directors of SharpLink.

data.title: Trump to Expand Retirement Investment Options Beyond Traditional Assets
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump is preparing to open cryptocurrency, gold, and private equity to the $9 trillion U.S. retirement market, potentially transforming how Americans manage their savings. Three informed sources revealed that Trump is expected to sign an executive order as early as this week, allowing 401k retirement plans to include alternative investments beyond traditional stocks and bonds. These investments will encompass a wide range of asset classes, from digital assets to metals, and funds focused on corporate acquisitions, private loans, and infrastructure deals. The executive order will instruct regulatory agencies to investigate the barriers faced by professional management funds in incorporating alternative investments for 401k savers.
